body_txt: For many people, the ideal dream is to get married, have children, and live in a nice house when they grow up, with a mailbox and a white picket fence. But sometimes, you get it, and it's not what you wanted at all. This trope refers to the archetype of a depressed middle-aged man working a Soul-Crushing Desk Job who's married but totally disengaged from family life. He's stuck in a never-ending humdrum routine of waking up, going to work, getting yelled at by his boss all day, coming home, drinking , watching TV, and going to bed. His wife is either equally depressed or having affairs on the side. They hardly notice each other anymore at best, and are always fighting at worst. Once upon a time, they might've been young and happy, but not in the present day. He has kids, usually at least two, but doesn't know how to connect with them. He might have had big dreams in his youth, of being a rockstar or a football player or something, but they vanished into the wind for one reason or another . In terms of appearance, he tends to be overweight (especially if he's an alcoholic ) and balding or with thinning hair, looking like he let himself go (he might've been Formerly Fit or quite handsome in his younger years ). He usually wears some sort of office uniform with a dress shirt and tie, and has a coffee cup in hand . He looks very tired and hunched over, and typically has a 5 o'clock shadow, as if he couldn't be bothered to shave most mornings. In many cases, his life is like this because he has a demanding job and a mean boss. His wife is usually a housewife and stay-at-home mom, so there's more pressure on him to keep the job, no matter how much he hates it, and bring home the bacon. So when he comes home, he can't muster the energy to spend time with his wife and kids, and all he feels like doing is watching TV and drinking. From his wife's perspective, it feels like he isn't trying at all in their marriage when she's also working hard to take care of their house and kids, but he feels like she's nagging him, which causes friction and arguments. With this character, it tends to vary how much the audience is meant to feel bad for him. Sometimes he's an apathetic husband and/or neglectful father who doesn't appreciate having a nice house, a stable job, a loving wife and beautiful children, and doesn't realize it until his wife is handing him divorce papers. Sometimes his wife is a nagging shrew, and his children are brats who make his life miserable and don't appreciate how hard he works to put food on the table. Sometimes it's nobody's fault, life just got very stressful and full of responsibilities when he and his wife weren't looking, and they're two decent people in a difficult spot who have become emotionally disconnected, but still love each other and their kids. In the best-case scenario, he may remember that he really does love his wife and kids and make more of an effort to be a family man, or at least make an effort to improve his life, like taking up a new hobby or finding a better job. In the worst-case scenarios, he may have a more extreme reaction like starting an affair, having a midlife crisis, getting a divorce, killing himself , committing Pater Familicide , or leaving to go get milk and cigarettes.
